rulings.Case Title: STOKES, CORY, ON BEHALF OF HIMSELF AND ALL OTHERS VS. CSE HOLDINGS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Application And Plaintiff'S Application For Order: A) Granting Preliminary Approval Of Class Settlement And Provisionally Certifying The Settlement Class; B) Approving Form And Manner Of Notice To Provide To The Proposed Settlement Class And Directing That A Neutral Third Party Give Such Notice To The Proposed Settlement Class; C) Approving Heffler Claims Group, A Neutral Thrid Party, As Claims Administrator; D) Setting A Hearing For Final Approval Of The Proposed Settlement And Award Of Attorneys' Fees And Costs To Class Counsel And Enhancement To Class Representative; E) ...
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Tuesday, September 9, 2014, Line 13, PLAINTIFF STOKES, CORY, ON BEHALF OF HIMSELF AND ALL OTHERS Notice Of Application And Plaintiff'S Application For Order: A) Granting Preliminary Approval Of Class Settlement And Provisionally Certifying The Settlement Class; B) Approving Form And Manner Of Notice To Provide To The Proposed Settlement Class And Directing That A Neutral Third Party Give Such Notice To The Proposed Settlement Class; C) Approving Heffler Claims Group, A Neutral Thrid Party, As Claims Administrator; D) Setting A Hearing For Final Approval Of The Proposed Settlement And Award Of Attorneys' Fees And Costs To Class Counsel And Enhancement To Class Representative; E) ... Continued to September 23, 2014. There are several pieces of information that the parties must provide before the Court can preliminarily approve the settlement agreement. Counsel has not provided data on how many workweeks each of the 57 Account Managers completed, nor has it provided the "distribution formula" to explain the value of each workweek. It is also unclear what will happen in the event that few people sign up and there are funds leftover from the Net Settlement Amount. It appears that the cy pres organization will only receive uncashed checks, not necessarily surplus funds (if any) from the Net Settlement Amount. It is unclear whether the settlement agreement to have the surplus funds be distributed among all class members, be passed to cy pres, or revert to Defendant. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =302/EHG

rulings.Case Title: MARK RITTENBERG et al VS. PG&E CORPORATION, A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Tuesday, September 9, 2014, Line 6, DEFENDANTS DENICE BRITTON, BRITTON TREE SERVICES, INC.'S, A CORPORATION MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT The motion for summary judgment is denied. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether defendant Britton Tree Service, Inc./Denice Britton breached its duty of care and whether that breach caused damage to plaintiffs. See declarations of Randall Frizzell, Joseph McNeil, and Denice Britton for defendant and declaration of Steve Bickford and deposition of David Isaacs pgs. 126-137 for plaintiff). The court overrules the parties' evidentiary objections. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/EHG

rulings.Case Title: ANTHONY MENTO VS. CYPRESS SECURITY LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Tuesday, September 9, 2014, Line 9, DEFENDANTS CYPRESS SECURITY LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED, CHRISTOPHER GRABARKIEWCTZ' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Sustained without leave to amend. The First Amended Complaint ("FAC") alleges that Plaintiff was retaliated against for engaging in discussions with a group of staff members related to the working conditions of all employees. (FAC, pars. 17, 21-22.) Such conduct is "arguably protected" under the National Labor Relations Act and is therefore preempted. (Luke v. Collotype Labels USA, Inc. (2008) 159 Cal.App.4th 1463, 1471.) Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted. Defendant has established good cause for why the document was not submitted until reply. (See Balboa Ins. Co. v. Aguirre (1983) 149 Cal.App.3d 1002, 1010.) Plaintiff clearly knew about the claim that was filed in May 2012 and is not prejudiced by the Court's consideration of the document. The document is judicially noticed pursuant to Cal. Evid. Code sec. 452(c). The Court may take judicial notice of the filing of the claim. (See Gong v. City of Rosemead (2014) 226 Cal.App.4th 363, 379, fn 1.) The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend because Plaintiff has not stated how he could amend the FAC to state a viable cause of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/EHG
